Transform Your Atlanta Kitchen

Bespoke kitchen remodels designed for Georgia homes. From historic bungalows to modern subdivisions, we create spaces that blend beauty with Southern functionality.

+ Years in Business
Projects Completed
Client Satisfaction
Team Members

Crafting Atlanta's Favorite Kitchens

Founded in 2012, Evergreen Kitchen & Bath began as a two-person operation serving Midtown's historic homes. Today, our team of 20+ specialists brings over a decade of Atlanta-specific remodeling expertise to every project.

We approach each kitchen as a unique puzzle - considering Georgia's humid climate, red clay soil shifts, and your family's gumbo-making traditions. Our designs balance timeless craftsmanship with smart storage solutions for Southern living.

When we're not installing custom cabinets, you'll find our team volunteering at Atlanta Community Food Bank or sponsoring Little Five Points festivals. These kitchens feed our community in more ways than one.

12+ Years Experience
800+ Projects Done
97% Satisfaction

Why Choose Evergreen Kitchen & Bath

Climate-Adaptive Materials

We specify humidity-resistant finishes and expansion-friendly installations to withstand Atlanta's seasonal shifts.

Local Design Insight

From Buckhead townhomes to Alpharetta McMansions, we understand how Atlantans actually use their kitchens.

Full-Service Coordination

Our project managers handle permits from Fulton County to Cobb County - you enjoy the process.

Heritage Home Specialists

We preserve original charm while updating Virginia-Highland bungalows with modern functionality.

What We Offer

Our Services

Kitchen Remodeling

Our full kitchen remodels honor your home's character while introducing contemporary convenience. For Midtown's historic bungalows, we might preserve original hardwood floors while installing custom Shaker cabinets with soft-close hinges to combat Georgia's humidity. In Johns Creek's newer developments, we often create open-concept layouts with quartz countertops that withstand busy family life. Atlanta's red clay soil demands special attention to cabinet leveling - our installers use premium shimming techniques to prevent shifting. We frequently incorporate deep pantries for Southern staple storage and pot-filler faucets positioned for gumbo prep. Every design includes ventilation solutions for those sizzling summer fry sessions.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: On-site consultation assessing your home's structure, climate considerations, and cooking habits
  2. 02 Step 2: 3D renderings showing layout options with Atlanta-appropriate traffic flow
  3. 03 Step 3: Careful demolition preserving reusable materials common in older Atlanta homes
  4. 04 Step 4: Installation with humidity-controlled adhesives and expansion gaps for Georgia seasons
View Full Details

What Our Clients Say

Evergreen transformed our 1940s Virginia-Highland bungalow kitchen while preserving its charm. They replicated original cabinet profiles but added soft-close hinges and pull-out shelves that make cooking so much easier. The team even matched our home's historic molding when creating the new breakfast nook.

Margaret T. Kitchen Remodeling

After two failed attempts with other contractors, Evergreen finally got our Johns Creek cabinet doors to stay aligned through Georgia's humidity swings. Their custom humidity-sealed storage solution for our spice collection is a game-changer for Indian cooking.

Raj P.

Our Marietta quartz counters survived a Thanksgiving disaster when a hot roasting pan was set directly on the surface - no stain or scorch mark! The team's precise installation left no gaps for crumbs to collect, and the integrated pastry marble slab is my baking secret weapon.

Lisa and Mark W.

As a chef, I needed an Atlanta kitchen that could handle both family meals and occasional catering prep. Evergreen's design includes a commercial-grade vent hood disguised as a beautiful focal point, and their custom knife storage is sharper than any restaurant I've worked in.

Carlos M.

The luxury vinyl plank Evergreen recommended for our Sandy Springs kitchen looks like hardwood but handles our two dogs' muddy paws effortlessly. Their subfloor prep made all the difference - no squeaks or gaps even after last winter's freeze.

Nia J.
